description: Canonical audit trail standard for Beast System 3.0. Defines event structure, chain requirements, immutability rules, metadata enrichment, validation logic, and cross-layer audit invariants.

field_definitions.event_type: Classification of the event (create, update, revoke, validate, finalize, discrepancy_detected, corrective_action).
field_definitions.actor_id: Identity or system responsible for the event.
field_definitions.context: Narrative or structured metadata describing why the event occurred.
field_definitions.hash: SHA-256 hash of the event object.
field_definitions.previous_hash: Hash of the previous event in the chain.

rules: Every event must contain a valid hash.
Every event must reference the previous event hash.
Any break in the chain invalidates the entire sequence.
Chain validation must occur after every append operation.
No event may be deleted or altered once written.

federal_timestamp_anchor.description: Indicates whether the event is anchored to a higher-rigor timestamp context.

lineage_reference.description: Optional lineage ID for events tied to identity or succession.

ethical_context.description: Ethical reasoning or constraints relevant to the event.

wellbeing_context.description: Wellbeing-related metadata for trauma-preventive or support actions.

rules: All validators must pass before an event is accepted.
Validation failures must be logged as discrepancy events.
Corrective-action workflow must trigger automatically on failure.
Corrective actions must be non-punitive.
Corrective actions must preserve chain integrity.
Corrective actions must generate their own audit events.
Corrective actions must not overwrite original events.
All discrepancies must be reviewed.
Discrepancies must be logged as separate events.
Discrepancy logs must be chained.
Discrepancy resolution must be documented.
Finalization must freeze the chain.
Finalized chains must be archived.
Finalized chains must be cross-linked to registry records.
Finalization must generate a federal-context timestamp if required.

identity_layer: All audit events must reference valid identity IDs.
Identity anchors must be validated before event acceptance.
registry_layer: All registry modifications must generate audit events.
Registry entries must reference audit chain IDs.
ethical_authority_layer: Events must not violate dignity or harm-reduction invariants.
Ethical flags must be included when applicable.
wellbeing_standards_layer: Events affecting people must include wellbeing context.
Stabilization and support actions must be logged.
lineage_layer: Lineage events must be chained and immutable.
Succession events must include lineage validation.

federal_context.notes: Audit Trail Standard defines the canonical audit invariants for Beast System 3.0 and must remain immutable within the fictional/organizational system.
